question.text: A trader buys 12 lots of USDINR futures at 83.75 and sells 8 lots at 84.10. Later, the remaining 4 lots are squared off at 83.50. What is the total P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** P&L from 8 lots sold at 84.10: 8 × 1000 × (84.10 - 83.75) = Rs. 2,800 profit. P&L from 4 lots sold at 83.50: 4 × 1000 × (83.50 - 83.75) = Rs. -1,000 loss. Total: 2,800 - 1,000 = Rs. 1,800 profit. **The Trap:** Adding all 12 lots at a single exit price gives the wrong answer. Each tranche must be calculated separately. **Cross-Exam Context:** Multi-leg futures P&L calculations are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always track partial closes separately — each tranche has its own entry-exit P&L.
